_note: Updated 2026-05-29 (local) / 2026-05-30 (UTC) cycle AQ overnight-triage. Critic participated via the closing METRIC_INTEGRITY_PROTOCOL  3.1 attestation AND an independent verification. Tonight was a heartbeat-only cycle (FIQ + bug-reports inbox both ABSENT for the 77th consecutive cycle, verified by directory-absence checks not waved off), so the three  3.1 concrete questions resolve N/A by absence: no bug-report diagnoses to scrutinize, no proposals to challenge, no FIQ grades to audit. Cycle AQ is a NEGATIVE-MOVEMENT cycle, the exact mirror of cycle AP: where AP rose 87.1->88.6 on the concurrent session committing its artifacts (A12 60->90), AQ FELL 87.8->87.1 (-0.7) as the concurrent session RESUMED editing and re-dirtied the tree (A12 75->60, yellow->RED). On a negative-movement night the failure modes are (a) hiding the drop or blaming triage, (b) inventing a code defect (e.g. 'the watcher is broken') to look diagnostic, and (c) manufacturing a remediation proposal for a self-resolving condition. The critic guarded against all three. Independent verification: demanded the engineer read git diff docs/reports/app-health.html verbatim rather than asserting '-0.7' -- the diff (baseline committed HEAD dae430db @ 87.8) shows overall_score 87.8->87.1, the A12_operational dimension score 75->60, status yellow->RED, label 'pipeline=yellow - 7 recent skip-dirty' -> 'pipeline=red - 10 recent skip-dirty', and the attention_item/agent_attention weak-point '7 of last 10' -> '10 of last 10 cron watcher runs hit skip-dirty'. So the -0.7 is PROVEN a single-dimension A12 RED transition (NOT a clock-only diff). The critic then forced the attribution to be EVIDENCE-GROUNDED, not defect-inventing: git status shows the concurrent feature session is still live (M src/pages/home-rail-newuser.js tracked edit + M .claude/state/emu-unified-2026-05-29.log emulator log), keeping the tree dirty across the downloads-watcher's last 10 runs, so 10/10 correctly hit skip-dirty. The watcher is behaving CORRECTLY by declining a dirty tree -- there is NO watcher bug, and the critic blocked any framing that invented one. KEY HONESTY MOVE: refused to author a remediation proposal, because the A12 dip is SELF-RESOLVING -- it recovered to 90 in AP the instant the concurrent session committed, and will recover again the same way; a proposal for a self-clearing concurrent-driven condition would be ship-count gaming per Rule 2. The critic confirmed the commit uses an explicit pathspec capturing ONLY this cycle's own files (engineer.json + critic.json + journal + the regen-output app-health.html) and does NOT sweep src/pages/home-rail-newuser.js or .claude/state/emu-unified-2026-05-29.log. The critic also validated the founder-checklist open=6 (red=0 yellow=4 green=2 closed=25) as concurrent-ship-driven and deterministic, red=0 so nothing ship-blocking -- flagged as awareness, not a movement. The critic actively guarded AGAINST hiding the drop, against inventing a watcher defect, against credit/blame misattribution, AND against manufacturing busywork. Critic token counter at ~209k cumulative since last rest (threshold crossed at cycle AH; +~7k this cycle); status remains active and no rest triggered -- heartbeat-only critic load is genuinely light per-cycle. Critic attests cleanly: substantive heartbeat cycle, ship closes.
